steps.title: Identify the transformation rules for shifts
steps.content:
When a graph is translated:
  • Right by $h$ units: replace $x$ with $(x - h)$
  • Down by $k$ units: subtract $k$ from the function value
Since the graph moves 4 units right and 3 units down, we need to apply both rules to $f(x)$.

steps.title: Apply the rightward shift of 4 units
steps.content:
Replace $x$ with $(x - 4)$ in the original function:
$$f(x - 4) = \frac{a-19}{x-4} + 5$$

steps.title: Apply the downward shift of 3 units
Graph a concrete value to check the choices
steps.content: Because $a$ is just a constant, use any convenient value to visualize the shift. If $a=23$, then $a-19=4$ and $a-22=1$. Graph the original representative function and the four choice forms. The choice B graph is the one shifted right $4$ and down $3$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The correct transformed function is $g(x)=\frac{a-19}{x-4}+2$, which matches choice B.
